core_payload.content:
# Key Agents Swarm Lane

## 1. Boundary
Key Agents Swarm Lane owns the background swarm roles that keep N1Hub moving outside the direct human conversation loop. This sub-hub exists so the N-Infinity role family and background runtime sit together as an explicit lane instead of blending into user, planning, or governance maps.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `n-infinity.weaver`, `n-infinity.gardener`, `n-infinity.parliament`, `n-infinity.innovator`, `n-infinity.suggestion-agent`, `n-infinity.reminder-agent`, `n-infinity.risk-detector`, and `background-agent-runtime`. Together they define the swarm roles that scan resonance, maintain graph ecology, arbitrate contradiction, shape options, monitor risk, apply reminders, and host longer-lived autonomous work.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own human-facing assistant surfaces, DeepMine extraction, planning compression, or the governance acceptance boundary. Those belong to `user-lane`, `mining-lane`, `planning-lane`, and `governance-lane`, while vault-maintenance execution remains under `stewardship-lane`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Key Agents Swarm Lane groups the background swarm roles of N1Hub: the N-Infinity family plus the background runtime substrate. It makes long-lived autonomous graph work explicit as its own lane instead of blending it into user, planning, or governance maps. This matters because night-shift execution, orchestration, and autonomous compounding need a visible home where runtime substrate and swarm roles can be reasoned about together without obscuring human-facing assistance or acceptance-boundary work.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of key-agents hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 28 outbound to ≤9.
